title: 2.9 Response in Plants
description: Response in plants — every fact from the textbook.

questions.question: What is one of the important characteristics of plants mentioned in the response section?
options: Ability to respond to stimuli (changes in the environment)
Ability to change colour at will
questions.correctAnswer: Ability to respond to stimuli (changes in the environment)

questions.question: What is tropism?
options: Bending towards or opposite to the direction of a stimulus
questions.correctAnswer: Bending towards or opposite to the direction of a stimulus

questions.question: What is positive tropism?
questions.correctAnswer: Bending towards the stimulus

questions.question: What is negative tropism?
questions.correctAnswer: Bending opposite to the stimulus

questions.question: What causes tropism?
options: Unilateral stimulus causing unequal production or distribution of growth hormone resulting in unequal growth
questions.correctAnswer: Unilateral stimulus causing unequal production or distribution of growth hormone resulting in unequal growth

questions.question: Which parts of a plant exhibit tropism?
questions.correctAnswer: The shoot and root

questions.question: What is the growth hormone commonly involved in tropism?
questions.correctAnswer: Auxin

questions.question: What is the specific name of the auxin involved in plant growth?
questions.correctAnswer: Indole Acetic Acid (IAA)

questions.question: Where is auxin produced?
options: At the tips of shoot and root
questions.correctAnswer: At the tips of shoot and root

questions.question: What does auxin affect?
questions.correctAnswer: Cell elongation

questions.question: What auxin concentration promotes shoot growth?
What effect does auxin concentration that promotes shoot growth have on roots?
questions.correctAnswer: Inhibits root growth

questions.question: What auxin concentration is required for root growth?
questions.correctAnswer: About $10^{-4}$ ppm

questions.question: What effect does the auxin concentration required for root growth have on shoot growth?
options: Has no effect on shoot growth
questions.correctAnswer: Has no effect on shoot growth

questions.question: In response to unilateral light, where does auxin move in the shoot and root?
options: From shaded side to illuminated side
From illuminated side to shaded side
questions.correctAnswer: From illuminated side to shaded side

questions.question: Why does the shoot bend towards light in response to unilateral light?
options: Higher auxin on illuminated side inhibits growth
Higher auxin on the shaded side causes more growth by cell elongation than the illuminated side
Auxin destroys cells on the illuminated side
questions.correctAnswer: Higher auxin on the shaded side causes more growth by cell elongation than the illuminated side

questions.question: Why does the root bend away from light?
options: More auxin on the shaded part of the root inhibits cell elongation compared to the illuminated side
The root is not affected by auxin
questions.correctAnswer: More auxin on the shaded part of the root inhibits cell elongation compared to the illuminated side

questions.question: What is phototropism?
options: Plant response to light, mainly exhibited by the shoot
questions.correctAnswer: Plant response to light, mainly exhibited by the shoot

questions.question: How does the shoot respond to light?
questions.correctAnswer: Positively phototropic (bends towards light)

questions.question: Who conducted the earliest experiment on phototropism?
questions.correctAnswer: Charles Darwin

questions.question: What is a coleoptile?
options: The shoot tip of a young seedling
questions.correctAnswer: The shoot tip of a young seedling

questions.question: What did Darwin observe about the bending in response to light?
options: The bending occurred in the tip itself
The bending did not occur in the tip but in the elongating part just below
The tip did not respond to light
questions.correctAnswer: The bending did not occur in the tip but in the elongating part just below

questions.question: What happened when the shoot tip was removed or covered with opaque foil in Darwin's experiment?
options: The shoot bent more towards light
The shoot could no longer bend toward the light
The shoot bent away from light
questions.correctAnswer: The shoot could no longer bend toward the light

questions.question: What did Darwin conclude from his phototropism experiment?
options: Light causes the tip to grow faster
Some influence is transmitted from the tip to more basal regions, regulating growth and inducing curvature
The tip directly bends toward light
questions.correctAnswer: Some influence is transmitted from the tip to more basal regions, regulating growth and inducing curvature

questions.question: What is hydrotropism?
options: Response to water, exhibited by roots
questions.correctAnswer: Response to water, exhibited by roots

questions.question: Why do roots grow toward water?
options: Because water is needed for photosynthesis and to support cell structure
questions.correctAnswer: Because water is needed for photosynthesis and to support cell structure

questions.question: What keeps cells turgid and strong?
questions.correctAnswer: Water

questions.question: What is geotropism?
questions.correctAnswer: Response to gravity

questions.question: How does the root respond to gravity?
questions.correctAnswer: Positively geotropic (grows downward towards gravity)

questions.question: How does the shoot respond to gravity?
questions.correctAnswer: Negatively geotropic (grows upward against gravity)

questions.question: What happens to a seedling placed horizontally?
options: The root bends downward while the shoot bends upward until the plant is upright
Both root and shoot grow upward
Both root and shoot grow downward
questions.correctAnswer: The root bends downward while the shoot bends upward until the plant is upright

questions.question: What instrument can overcome the effect of gravity on a horizontally placed seedling?
questions.correctAnswer: Clinostat

questions.question: What is a clinostat?
options: A rotating instrument that overcomes the effect of gravity
A tool for measuring water content
questions.correctAnswer: A rotating instrument that overcomes the effect of gravity

questions.question: What happens to a seedling fixed horizontally in a rotating clinostat?
options: It grows straight because rotation overcomes gravity
questions.correctAnswer: It grows straight because rotation overcomes gravity
